name: issues
context: Create, search, update, and manage Linear issues via the GraphQL API and TypeScript SDK
info: This skill covers the full issue lifecycle in Linear: creating issues with titles, descriptions, assignees, labels, and priorities; searching and filtering issues with GraphQL queries; updating issue state, assignees, and properties; and bulk operations. Issues are the atomic unit of work in Linear — every bug, feature, and task is an issue. Issues belong to exactly one team and move through team-specific workflow states. The @linear/sdk provides typed methods for all operations. Sub-skills [[linear/issues/create]] and [[linear/issues/update]] provide focused coverage. See [[linear/auth]] for authentication and [[linear/teams]] for team context.

sections.title: Querying issues
sections.body: Fetch a single issue by UUID or identifier (e.g., `ENG-42`) with `issue(id)`. List issues with `issues(filter, first, after, orderBy)`. Filter by team, state type, priority, assignee, labels, dates. Paginate with cursor-based Relay pagination using `pageInfo.hasNextPage` and `pageInfo.endCursor`. Search text with `searchIssues(query)`.

sections.title: Issue fields reference
sections.body: Required: `title` and `teamId` (for creation). Key optional fields: `description` (markdown), `assigneeId`, `stateId`, `priority` (0-4, where 1 is urgent), `labelIds`, `projectId`, `cycleId`, `estimate`, `dueDate` (YYYY-MM-DD), `parentId` (for sub-issues). All IDs are UUIDs except `identifier` which is the human-readable key like `ENG-42`.

sections.title: Filter syntax
sections.body: GraphQL filters use nested comparators: `eq` (exact), `neq` (not equal), `in` (list), `contains`/`startsWith` (string), `lt`/`lte`/`gt`/`gte` (comparison), `null` (boolean). Filters can nest into relations: `assignee: { email: { contains: "@co.com" } }`. Combine multiple filters with AND logic. Use `includeArchived: true` to include archived issues.

links.context: Authentication required for all issue operations
Issues belong to teams — teamId is required
Issues can be grouped into projects
React to issue events in real time

related.note: Authentication required before any issue operation
Must know teamId to create issues
GitHub Issues is an alternative — Linear can sync with GitHub issues bidirectionally

scripts.name: Manage Linear Issues
scripts.description: List, search, and display Linear issues with filtering and pagination

LINEAR_TEAM_KEY.description: Team key to filter issues (e.g., ENG)

depends.reason: Authentication required for all issue API calls
